Patch Set 8:

(3 comments)

> There are still a few changelog users that do not get unregistered
 > because some test functions still use "copytool_cleanup()" /
 > "cleanup()". If this is an issue I can rebase on top of
 > https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/30098.

Does this cause test failures in other scripts that are not being run here because of "trivial"?  If not, then I'd prefer to land this patch first, so that https://review.whamcloud.com/30943 can use the changelog functionality added in this patch

Patch Set 8:

(3 comments)

> Does this cause test failures in other scripts that are not being run here because of "trivial"?

I cannot say for sure. I beleive sanity.sh only ever references changelog users its tests registered, but if there is a global test like "register -> unregister -> check absolutely no user is still registered", it will probably fail if run after sanity-hsm.

On the other hand, if Maloo umounts the FS between test suites, I don't think there can be any issue. Does it do so?

If this is too much of an issue, I can easily rebase the change on top of this one: https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/30098/17. After doing so, changelog users can be expected to be cleaned correctly in every case. (I did not do it yet to give them both equal chances to land first)

parents.subject: LU-8130 ldlm: store name directly in namespace.

commit.subject: LU-10796 tests: standardize changelog testing in sanity-hsm
commit.message:
LU-10796 tests: standardize changelog testing in sanity-hsm

To manage changelog users and changelog records, sanity-hsm used to
define:
 - changelog_setup
 - changelog_cleanup
 - changelog_get_flags

test-framework.sh implements similar functions:
 - changelog_register
 - changelog_deregister
 - changelog_dump
 - changelog2array (new in this patch)

This patch removes the implementations of sanity-hsm in favor of
those in test-framework.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanity-hsm
Signed-off-by: Quentin Bouget <quentin.bouget@cea.fr>
Change-Id: Ie3db8ef646fa48d06bf41b6025b3443de026cabd
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/31613
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
